payload.answer: Treat this as a temporal status transition/update. Preserve the existing project identity, but do not overwrite the broader project as fully implemented unless separately confirmed. Record that the Brain Slack agent MVP is implemented; the earlier planned/open state is older and should not be presented as current for the MVP scope.
citations: Existing: "The Brain Slack agent is planned, status=open."
New: "The Brain Slack agent MVP is now implemented."

entity_resolution.reason: The new statement refers to the MVP of the existing Brain Slack agent project and indicates a later implementation state for that scoped milestone, not a separate unrelated entity.

memory_cards.statement: The Brain Slack agent MVP is now implemented.

details: Detected same project lineage with narrower scope in the new statement ('MVP').
Classified the difference as a time-based status progression rather than a hard contradiction.
Avoided promoting the whole Brain Slack agent to fully implemented because only the MVP is explicitly confirmed.

repair_options: Update the project record to note that the MVP milestone is implemented while leaving overall project status unspecified unless confirmed.
If the system requires a single project status, mark the previous open/planned status as superseded by a later implementation update for the MVP scope.
Ask for clarification only if the data model cannot represent milestone-level status separately from overall project status.
